data.title: Atlas World Sports Names Kevin Koeppel Director of Product to Lead Fantasy Sports Expansion
data.description:
CHICAGO, July 17, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Atlas World Sports, the rapidly growing sports betting and fantasy sports platform, has named Kevin Koeppel as its new Director of Product. The announcement comes as the company prepares to launch AtlasDFS.com, under the Atlas World Sports brand.

Kevin Koeppel

Koeppel, a proven entrepreneur in the fantasy sports space, will lead product strategy and development for Atlas' expanding fantasy division. He previously founded No House Advantage, a DFS platform that offered real-money contests across all major professional sports leagues. That company was acquired in 2021 and later transitioned into Betr, the micro-betting-focused sportsbook launched by Jake Paul in 2023.

"Kevin brings deep product expertise and a unique understanding of both DFS and user behavior," said Robert Kraft, CEO and Co-Founder of Atlas World Sports. "His track record of launching and scaling fantasy products makes him the ideal person to lead this next phase of our growth."

The appointment aligns with Atlas World Sports' broader vision of becoming the go-to destination for sports fans who bet, watch, and play fantasy. Following the acquisition of the popular DFS platform, Lockspread, Atlas now offers a seamless experience that combines odds aggregation from over 20 sportsbooks with predictive AI, live stats, expert insights, and unique fantasy game formats--all in one app.

"I'm thrilled to join Atlas World Sports at such a pivotal time," said Koeppel. "Our goal is to build a next-generation fantasy sports experience that blends everything fans love about season-long and daily fantasy with the real-time power of sports betting insights."

Atlas World Sports is often described as the "Expedia for Sportsbooks," helping users compare odds, promotions, and bet types across multiple platforms. The app integrates with major operators like DraftKings, FanDuel, Caesars, BetMGM, and Hard Rock, and leverages AI to personalize recommendations, surface value bets, and send smart alerts.

With partnerships already in place with the NBA, NFL, MLB, PGA, UFC, and more, the company recently secured investment from Starkel Capital Management as part of its ongoing Series A fundraising, positioning it for continued growth in both the sports betting and fantasy verticals.

For more information, visit www.atlasworldsports.com

Contact: gary@atlasworldsports.com

SOURCE Atlas World Sports

data.title: resolves public share buyback offer at a price of € 1.90 per share
data.description:
7C Solarparken AG

by Koen Boriau

Public disclosure of inside information according to article 17 MAR

7C Solarparken AG: resolves public share buyback offer at a price of € 1.90 per share

Bayreuth (pta000/17.07.2025/18:50 UTC+2)

The Management Board of 7C Solarparken AG, on the basis of the authorisation granted by the Annual General Meeting on 4 June 2025 to repurchase shares in accordance with Section 71 (1) No. 8 German Stock Corporation Law, resolved today, with the approval of the company's Supervisory Board, to repurchase up to 1,600,000 shares of 7C Solarparken AG (approx. 2% of the share capital) as part of a voluntary public share buyback offer addressed to all shareholders.

The acceptance period begins on 22 July 2025 and ends on 11 August 2025, subject to an extension. During this period, the company's shareholders can tender their shares to 7C Solarparken AG at a purchase price (excluding incidental acquisition costs) of EUR 1.90 per share. The purchase price corresponds to a premium of around 6 % on the relevant average price on the ten trading days prior to the resolution.

The share repurchase tender document will be published shortly on the company's website at https://www.solarparken.com in the Investor Relations – Share Data – "Public share repurchase offer" section as well as in the German Federal Gazette (www.bundesanzeiger.de).

The current share buyback programme 2025 will be suspended during the acceptance period of the public share buyback offer. With the buyback, the company aims to increase the value per share and still has sufficient financial resources for further growth.

(end)

data.title: SF FED'S DALY Q&A/BBG: GOODS PRICES INFLATION IN CPI SHOWED EFFECT OF TARIFFS BUT OTHER PRICES 'COMING DOWN;' CAN'T 'HOLD THE BRIDLE TOO TIGHT'
Jamie Dimon overstretches his fintech flex
data.description:
JPMorgan’s boss plans to charge aggregators like Yodlee and Plaid for basic user info while trying to kill new data-sharing rules. With big digital expenses and $34 bln of card fees to consider, the push makes sense. It also risks drawing attention to the bank's market power.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow Jeffrey Goldfarb on X and Linkedin.

CONTEXT NEWS

JPMorgan has told financial-technology firms that it will start charging fees for access to customer bank account information that is used to send and receive money from payments, trading and other outside service providers, Bloomberg reported on July 11, citing unnamed sources.

The Bank Policy Institute, along with a bank and a state trade group on May 30 asked a U.S. court to throw out the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s Section 1033 rule, which would, among other things, prohibit banks from charging fees to third-party entities for accessing customer account information. Plaintiffs allege that the CFPB overstepped its statutory authority and approved burdensome regulation.

The agency itself, which has been pared back under President Donald Trump, also filed documents with the court saying it has concluded the rule exceeds its authority. The Financial Technology Association has been granted permission by the court to step in and defend the rule.

data.title: July Homebuilder Confidence Picks Up Following Passage of Trump's Tax Bill
data.description:
US homebuilder confidence edged up in July, buoyed by the passage of President Donald Trump's tax and spending legislation, according to National Association of Home Builders and Wells Fargo data released Thursday.

The housing market index, which covers new single-family homes, rose by one point sequentially to 33, which was the consensus in a survey compiled by Bloomberg. Last month's reading was the lowest since December 2022.

"The passage of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act provided a number of important wins for households, home builders and small businesses," NAHB Chair Buddy Hughes said in a statement.

The tax and spending legislation, signed into law on July 4, prevents the end-of-year tax hike and expands the low-income housing tax credit to incentivize the construction of affordable homes.

Elevated interest rates along with economic uncertainty, however, tempered homebuilders' optimism.

"While this new law should provide economic momentum after a disappointing spring, the housing sector has weakened in 2025 due to poor affordability conditions, particularly from elevated interest rates," Hughes said.

About 38% of builders cut home prices this month, the highest level since the NAHB began tracking this index on a monthly basis. Prices were reduced by an average 5%, unchanged from June.

The index gauging current sales conditions rose to 36 this month from 35 in June. Sales expectations over the next six months increased three points to 43, while the measure charting prospective buyers dropped by one point to 20, the NAHB survey showed.

data.title: Texas School District Selling $103 Million of Bonds for Construction Projects
data.description:
By Patrick Sheridan

Alvin Independent School District of Brazoria County, Texas is offering approximately $103 million of bonds for the construction and equipping of new school buildings.

The Series 2025 A Unlimited Tax Schoolhouse Bonds mature from 2026 through 2053, according to a preliminary official statement posted Wednesday on MuniOS. Dates of sale and delivery of the securities wasn't provided.

Interest on the bonds will be payable starting on Aug. 15 and again on Feb. 15.

The bonds are direct obligations of the district and are backed by money collected from an annual ad valorem tax levied, without legal limit as to rate or amount, against all taxable property located within the district.

Proceeds will be used for the construction of school buildings and the purchase of land for the construction of new schools. Money from the sale will also be used to pay issuance costs.

Located south of Houston on the gulf coast, the district operates 36 schools and had about 30,000 students enrolled for the 2024-2025 school year.

The district has applied for ratings on the securities from Moody's and Fitch Ratings. The district has also applied and expects to receive approval for backing on the debt from the state's Permanent School Fund Guarantee Program, which acts would step in to pay bond holders in the event of a default. The fund has underlying ratings of Aa2 from Moody's and AA from Fitch.

Piper Sandler is the underwriter of the offering.

Write to Patrick Sheridan at patrick.sheridan@wsj.com

data.title: Nymex Overview: Oil Rebounds on Bargain Hunting, Lingering Tensions — OPIS
data.description:
Crude oil rose moderately in midday Thursday trade after three consecutive daily losses, boosted by bargain hunting and supply worries amid uncertain U.S. policies toward Russia related to the Ukraine war.

At noon ET, more-active September Nymex West Texas Intermediate crude contracts were 60cts higher at $65.80/bbl and August WTI was up around 70cts at $67.10/bbl.

London-based September ICE Brent contracts rose about 50cts to $69/bbl and October Brent was up 55cts at $68.10/bbl.

Crude oil prices fell around $1/bbl during their three days of losses.

Diesel prices outperformed gasoline. September Nymex ULSD jumped 5.3cts to $2.4195/gal and August ULSD was 5.75cts higher at $2.449/gal. September RBOB climbed 1.2cts to $2.1135/gal and August RBOB was up by 1ct at $2.154/gal.

On Monday, a Washington proposal to impose harsh tariffs against Russia initially stirred oil supply risks and lifted oil futures. However, petroleum prices quickly retreated after President Trump said Moscow had 50 days to negotiate a plan to end the Ukraine war.

In the cash market, most products tracked Nymex futures to trade higher.

Spot participants monitored possible operational issues after Phillps 66 said Monday's heavy rain caused a partial power outage at its 272,135 b/d Bayway refinery in Linden, N.J. In addition, Chevron said a fire broke out Monday at the wastewater treatment area of its 375,200 b/d Pascagoula, Miss., refinery, injuring four contract workers.

data.title: Wheat Under Pressure on Supportive North American Weather — Market Talk
data.description: CBOT wheat is down 1% in afternoon trading, leading the way lower for the grains complex. "Wheat is seeing some pressure from wetter forecasts for the U.S. Plains up into Canada," says Karl Setzer of Consus Ag Consulting. The U.S. Drought Monitor data show dry conditions further dissipating in Plains states like Nebraska and South Dakota. Eastern Corn Belt states like Iowa and Missouri are essentially drought-free, according to the Drought Monitor. Setzer adds that soybeans on the CBOT are up today due to supportive crushing data from the National Oilseed Processors Association issued this week. Soybeans rise 0.7% and corn is down 0.1%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: Traders digest Angolan September loadings
data.description:
West African crude oil differentials held steady on Thursday as market participants digested an Angolan loading programme for September.

* Angola will load a total of 32 crude cargoes in September, averaging around 1.03 million bpd.

* That compares with 31 cargoes scheduled for August at 965,000 bpd.

* Participants will now await the release of term allocations for September Angolan barrels.

* Demand for September-loading cargoes in Asia, which would arrive in the region in October, could be curbed by the autumn refinery maintenance season, a trader said.

* That could see sellers increasingly target U.S. or European buyers, the trader added.

* West African differentials could also come under pressure from falling values of rival sweet crudes. Azeri BTC was offered down to dated plus $2.55 CIF Augusta in the Platts window on Thursday, from dated plus $3.20 earlier this week.

* Recent offers for West African grades included Bonny Light at dated Brent plus $2.60 and Forcados at dated Brent plus $4.25, a trading source said earlier this week.

data.title: FDA advisers vote against combination therapies of GSK's blood cancer drug
data.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration's panel of independent advisers voted on Thursday against approval of GSK's blood cancer drug Blenrep for use in combination with other treatments.

data.title: IDACORP Schedules Second Quarter 2025 Earnings Release & Conference Call
data.description:
BOISE, Idaho--(BUSINESS WIRE)--July 17, 2025--

IDACORP, Inc. will report its second quarter results on Thursday, July 31, in a news release before the stock markets open. The company will hold an analyst conference call that day at 2:30 p.m. Mountain Time (4:30 p.m. Eastern Time) to discuss the second quarter 2025 earnings.

All parties interested in listening may do so through a live Webcast or by calling 855--761--5600 for listen-only mode. The passcode for the call is 9290150. The conference call logistics are posted on the company's Website (www.idacorpinc.com) and will be included in the company's earnings news release. Slides will be included during the conference call. To access the slide deck, please visit www.idacorpinc.com/investor-relations. A replay of the conference call will be available on the company's website for a period of 12 months and will be available shortly after the call.

About IDACORP, INC.

IDACORP, Inc. is based in Boise, Idaho, and was formed in 1998 as a holding company. IDACORP subsidiaries include Idaho Power, a regulated energy company; IDACORP Financial, an investor in affordable housing projects and real estate; and Ida-West Energy, an operator of small hydroelectric projects. IDACORP's origins lie with Idaho Power and operations beginning in 1916. Today, Idaho Power employs approximately 2,100 people who serve more than 650,000 customers throughout a 24,000-square-mile area in southern Idaho and eastern Oregon. data.title: AMC Stock Pops. A Box-Office Rebound Could Bring More Gains. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Mariapaula Gonzalez

AMC Entertainment Holdings stock has been steadily rising, and one Wall Street analyst sees no end to the good times.

Shares surged 5.2% on Thursday, putting the stock on pace for its highest close since June 6, according to Dow Jones Market Data. Over the past three months, the stock has climbed nearly 22%. The S&P 500 is flat.

Benchmark analyst Mike Hickey raised his forecast for the movie chain's earnings, citing "outsized potential upside." Hickey said in a research note that he expects rising moviegoing demand and this year's box-office rebound will attract visitors and bring in revenue with higher margins. He also praised AMC's emphasis on customer loyalty through its membership programs and its use of premium screens like IMAX, which enhance the experience of visiting a theater.

"The exhibition landscape is showing signs of stabilization following pandemic and Hollywood strike disruptions, and AMC's positioning at the high end of the market should help it retain share as production volumes normalize and theatrical windows are reinforced," Hickey wrote in a Wednesday research note. He reaffirmed a Hold rating on the stock.

Hits like Superman, F1 and Jurassic World Rebirth have pushed summer ticket sales to surpass last year's totals by 16.5% so far, according to Sunday data from the media analytics firm Comscore. So far this year, total domestic box office revenue is $4.7 billion, 15% higher than it was at this time last year.

Hickey anticipates AMC's domestic ticket sales will increase by 37% year over year in the second quarter, which is well above his prior estimate of 12.5%. He also forecast revenue will increase to $1.3 billion, up from his previous call for $1.1 billion. Last quarter, AMC reported $862.5 million in revenue, down from $951.4 million in last year's first quarter.

The movie chain will report its second-quarter earnings before the market opens on August 8.

Benchmark isn't the only one who is more upbeat about the movie theater chain. Last week, a team of Wedbush analysts led by Alicia Reese upgraded AMC stock to Outperform from Neutral. It was the first rating equivalent to Buy in more than five years.

With coming releases like Smurfs and The Fantastic Four: First Steps — updates of tried-and-true franchises that are likely to bring in customers — AMC could continue to pop.

Write to editors@barrons.com.
